sections.heading: Purpose
sections.content:
This protocol is followed by the implementer (agent or human) after completing a feature or backlog item. It ensures documentation stays current and doc-to-code links are established before the work is considered done.

The protocol is intentionally short. Use `cortex_update_section` to patch individual sections without constructing full DocJSON.

sections.heading: Where Status Lives
sections.content:
Implementation status is tracked in two places:

| Doc Type | Section | Format | What it tracks |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Sub-feature PRD** `sub_features/*/prd.json` | `current-capabilities` | Status table: Done / In progress / Not started | Per-capability build status ŌĆö **the single source of truth** |
| **Interface specs** `interfaces/*.json` | `commands` / `tools` | Reference tables | Canonical source for CLI flags, API shapes |

The **capabilities table is the master status.** If a capability says Done, it is done. The backlog section is an append-only narrative log ŌĆö do not use it as a status tracker.

The **design spec** describes what exists (architecture, sequences, decisions). Update it when the implementation changes the system structure. There is no separate user guide until the project has external users.

sections.heading: Update Checklist
sections.content:
Follow this order after completing code for a feature or backlog item. Use `cortex_update_section` to patch sections directly ŌĆö pass the section ID and new content as markdown.

### Step 1: Sub-feature PRD capabilities table
**Section:** `current-capabilities`
**Action:** Change the Status column to `Done` for completed capabilities.
**Tool:** `cortex_update_section(section_id=..., content=<updated table>)`

### Step 2: Interface specs (if applicable)
**File:** `docs/features/{feature}/interfaces/*.json`
**Action:** Add new commands, flags, or API endpoints. Remove deprecated ones. This is the canonical reference for CLI/API shapes.

### Step 3: Design spec (if architecture changed)
**Section:** `architecture` or `sequence-diagram`
**Action:** Update if the implementation changed the system structure or added new flows. Add decision log entries for trade-offs made during implementation.
**Tool:** `cortex_update_section(section_id=..., content=<updated prose>)`

### Step 4: Add doc-to-code links
**Tool:** `cortex_add_link(section_id=..., node_id=...)`
**Decision test:** If a developer rewrites the linked function, would this section need review? If yes, link it.
**What to link:** Public entry points named in the prose, functions whose contract (inputs, outputs, behavior) is explicitly documented. See the linking policy in `cortex::docs.templates.audit-protocol::linking-policy` for detailed rules on private functions and contract boundaries.
**What NOT to link:** Private helpers (unless the section documents their internals), modules mentioned only for orientation, functions documented elsewhere.

### Step 5: Feature-scoped audit
Run a feature-scoped audit per `cortex::docs.templates.audit-protocol`. This replaces the previous ad-hoc verification step.

1. `cortex_build` ŌĆö re-index so changes are in the graph
2. `cortex_check` ŌĆö verify zero `DESC_STALE` on updated sections
3. `cortex_list_undocumented` ŌĆö confirm new code nodes have `documents` edges
4. Check workflow step markers on new/changed functions (see `cortex::docs.templates.audit-protocol::workflow-audit`)
5. `cortex history checkpoint` ŌĆö mark the implementation as a reference point for future "since" queries

sections.heading: Quick Reference Checklist
sections.content:
```
## Post-Implementation: [feature/backlog item name]
- [ ] Sub-feature PRD: updated capabilities table to Done
- [ ] Interface spec: added/removed commands, flags, options (if applicable)
- [ ] Design spec: updated architecture/decisions (if architecture changed)
- [ ] Doc-to-code links added (per linking policy in audit protocol)
- [ ] cortex_build ŌĆö completed without errors
- [ ] cortex_check ŌĆö zero DESC_STALE on updated sections
- [ ] cortex_list_undocumented ŌĆö no gaps for this feature
- [ ] Workflow step markers verified on new/changed functions
- [ ] cortex history checkpoint ŌĆö reference point recorded
```
